mapping.a_element: the INC endofunctor T_INC iterated to build the tower D, TD, T^2D, ... of tangent unfoldings
mapping.b_element: the doctor-proposing cumulative offer process iterated along its trajectories of contract offers

mapping.a_element: the tangent functor T acting as a structure-preserving (monotone) operator on the learning sketch
mapping.b_element: the monotone offer/choice operator induced by substitutable, irrelevance-of-rejected-contracts choice functions

mapping.a_element: the coalgebraic greatest/terminal fixed point nu-T_INC at which successive tangent unfoldings stabilize
mapping.b_element: the greatest stable allocation, i.e. the greatest element of the finite lattice, at which the offer-process trajectory terminates

mapping.a_element: Aczel-Mendler theorem guaranteeing existence of the final INC coalgebra
mapping.b_element: the lattice/Tarski-style guarantee that the finite lattice of weakly hospital-quasi-stable allocations has a stable greatest element

mapping.a_element: stabilization of the iterated tangent lifts as reaching the fixed point
mapping.b_element: termination of the cumulative offer process at the fixed-point (doctor-optimal) allocation

brief_justification: Both are greatest-fixed-point constructions: a monotone / structure-preserving operator is iterated until it stabilizes at its greatest (terminal) fixed point, whose existence is secured by a named fixed-point theorem -- Aczel-Mendler final-coalgebra existence (nu-T_INC) in A and the Tarski-style greatest element of the stable-allocation lattice reached by the cumulative offer process in B. Final coalgebras generalize order-theoretic greatest fixed points, so the same nu-fixed-point machinery drives both despite A living in tangent-category/ML language and B in many-to-many matching theory.
